mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through increased corporate earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138467.0 as of May 2013 reflects post-recession recovery trajectory
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth months do not produce statistically significantly higher 20-day forward returns than negative payroll months (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate above 4.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on future equity cash flows and raises corporate borrowing costs, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.49% as of May 2013, near trigger threshold
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive policy stance
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts (HOUST) combined with accelerating retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening aggregate demand and consumer confidence, jointly driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The interaction captures demand-side momentum that single-series proxies miss.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1229.0 and RSAFS at 385753.0 as of April 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with synchronized demand acceleration
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or the joint signal produces lower 20-day returns than either component alone in > 50% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month by more than 0.5% signal supply-side weakness and potential demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings downward in response to capacity utilization deterioration.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances following INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings expectations decline and recession risk increases.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% as of May 2013, below trigger threshold
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 70% of instances when UNRATE > 5.0%, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window during elevated unemployment regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.0%, indicating real rates are negative and monetary policy is accommodative. This regime produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as equity valuations expand under financial repression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 210.565 and FEDFUNDS at 4.49% as of May 2013
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with positive real rate compression
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day returns during accommodative regimes are not significantly higher than during restrictive regimes (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows acceleration above 2.5% annualized rate, indicating robust economic expansion that supports corporate profit growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11659.3 billion chained 2017 dollars as of Q1 2013
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns during above-2.5% growth quarters are not significantly higher than below-2.5% quarters (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month by more than 1.0% signals consumer spending weakness and demand destruction, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices consumer discretionary earnings and signals potential recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 385753.0 millions of dollars as of April 2013
regime_hint: demand-side contraction or consumer spending shock
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances following RSAFS declines > 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
